title: Set up Vitest + React Testing Library with mocks and config
description: Configure Vitest as the unit test runner with React Testing Library, happy-dom environment, global setup file, path alias resolution, and common mocks (next/navigation, next/image, zustand, ResizeObserver). Ensure coverage reporting via v8 provider.
acceptanceCriteria: vitest.config.ts created with happy-dom environment and path aliases
setup.ts registers global mocks (ResizeObserver, matchMedia, IntersectionObserver)
A sample component test passes via `pnpm test`
Coverage report generates to coverage/ directory with v8 provider

title: Unit tests for canvas-store (addNode, removeNodes, updateNodeData, undo/redo)
description: Write comprehensive unit tests for the canvas Zustand store covering node CRUD operations, edge management, and the undo/redo history stack. Test idempotency and boundary conditions.
acceptanceCriteria: addNode creates a node with correct default position and data
removeNodes deletes target nodes and their connected edges
updateNodeData merges partial data without overwriting unrelated fields
Undo reverses the last action; redo re-applies it; stack limits enforced

title: Unit tests for simulation-store (play/pause/stop, tick stepping, metrics)
description: Test the simulation store's state machine transitions (idle -> playing -> paused -> stopped), tick-based stepping logic, speed multiplier, and metrics aggregation after each tick.
acceptanceCriteria: play() transitions from idle/paused to playing state
pause() and stop() transition correctly and reset tick counter on stop
Tick stepping increments simulation time and updates node metrics
Speed multiplier correctly scales tick interval

title: Unit tests for ui-store (module switching, panel toggles, theme, persistence)
description: Test the UI store covering active module switching, sidebar/properties/bottom panel toggle states, theme switching (dark/light/system), and localStorage persistence hydration.
acceptanceCriteria: setActiveModule updates the current module and resets panel state if configured
Panel toggle actions (sidebar, properties, bottom) flip boolean state correctly
Theme preference persists to localStorage and hydrates on init
Persistence round-trip: store -> localStorage -> fresh store yields identical state

title: Unit tests for viewport-store
description: Test the viewport store covering zoom level clamping (min/max), pan offset updates, fitView calculations, and viewport reset to default.
acceptanceCriteria: Zoom clamps between configured min (0.1) and max (2.0) values
Pan offset updates correctly for x/y deltas
fitView calculates correct zoom and offset for a given node bounding box
resetViewport restores default zoom (1.0) and offset (0, 0)

title: Unit tests for editor-store
description: Test the editor store covering file open/close, active tab switching, dirty state tracking, and content update operations.
acceptanceCriteria: Opening a file creates a new tab with correct content and metadata
Closing a tab removes it and activates the nearest sibling
Editing content marks the tab as dirty; saving clears dirty flag
Active tab switches correctly when multiple tabs are open

title: Unit tests for interview-store
description: Test the interview store covering session creation, timer countdown, question navigation, answer submission, scoring calculation, and session history persistence.
acceptanceCriteria: Starting a session initializes timer, question index, and empty answers
Timer countdown decrements correctly and triggers timeout at zero
Question navigation (next/prev) updates index within bounds
Score calculation produces correct aggregate from individual question scores

title: Unit tests for queuing-model engine (M/M/1, M/M/c, Little's Law, edge cases)
description: Test the queuing theory math engine for M/M/1 and M/M/c models. Validate average wait time, queue length, utilization, and Little's Law identity (L = lambda * W). Cover edge cases: rho >= 1, zero arrival rate, single server.
acceptanceCriteria: M/M/1 with known inputs produces expected Wq, Lq, and utilization within 0.001 tolerance
M/M/c with c=3 matches reference values from queuing theory textbook
Little's Law identity L = lambda * W holds for all generated scenarios
Edge case: rho >= 1 returns Infinity or throws descriptive error

title: Unit tests for traffic-simulator (all 5 patterns, distributions, edge cases)
description: Test the traffic simulator engine for all five traffic patterns: constant, linear ramp, sinusoidal, spike burst, and random walk. Validate generated request counts match expected distributions and handle edge cases like zero duration and negative rates.
acceptanceCriteria: Constant pattern generates uniform RPS within 1% tolerance over 100 ticks
Sinusoidal pattern peaks and troughs match configured amplitude and period
Spike burst pattern fires correct burst count at configured intervals
Zero duration and negative rate inputs return empty arrays or throw validation errors

title: Unit tests for chaos-engine (30 event types, inject/remove/expire)
description: Test the chaos engine covering all 30 event types (network partition, latency spike, node crash, disk full, etc.). Validate inject applies the fault, remove clears it, and time-based expiry auto-removes after TTL.
acceptanceCriteria: Each of the 30 event types can be injected and produces the expected fault signature
remove() clears an active fault and restores prior state
TTL-based expiry auto-removes faults after configured duration
Injecting duplicate event type on same target is idempotent or returns conflict error

title: Unit tests for capacity-planner (reference inputs, formatEstimate)
description: Test the capacity planner module with known reference inputs (e.g., Twitter-scale, Uber-scale) and validate compute/storage/bandwidth estimates. Test the formatEstimate utility for human-readable output.
acceptanceCriteria: Reference input for 500M DAU produces compute estimate within expected range
Storage estimate scales linearly with data-per-user * user-count
formatEstimate converts bytes to human-readable (KB, MB, GB, TB, PB)
Edge case: zero users returns zero for all resource estimates

title: Unit tests for metrics-collector (circular buffer, percentiles)
description: Test the metrics collector's circular buffer implementation for fixed-size sample storage, correct overwrite on overflow, and accurate percentile calculations (p50, p95, p99).
acceptanceCriteria: Circular buffer stores up to maxSize samples and overwrites oldest on overflow
p50 of [1..100] returns 50; p95 returns 95; p99 returns 99
Empty buffer returns 0 or undefined for all percentile queries
Buffer reset clears all samples and resets write pointer

title: Unit tests for bubble-sort (empty, single, sorted, reverse, duplicates)
description: Test the bubble sort algorithm implementation against canonical edge cases: empty array, single element, already sorted, reverse sorted, all duplicates, and large random input for correctness.
acceptanceCriteria: Empty array returns empty array with zero comparisons
Single element returns same array unchanged
Already-sorted input returns identical array with minimal swaps
Reverse-sorted input produces correctly sorted output

title: Unit tests for merge-sort
description: Test the merge sort algorithm with the same canonical edge cases as bubble sort, plus verify stability (equal elements retain original order) and step-count recording for visualization.
acceptanceCriteria: All canonical edge cases (empty, single, sorted, reverse, duplicates) pass
Merge sort is stable: equal elements preserve input order
Step history records every merge operation for animation playback
Large input (10,000 elements) sorts correctly within performance budget

title: Unit tests for quick-sort
description: Test the quick sort algorithm covering edge cases, pivot selection strategies (first, last, median-of-three, random), worst-case inputs, and step recording for visualization.
acceptanceCriteria: All canonical edge cases pass for each pivot strategy
Median-of-three pivot avoids O(n^2) on already-sorted input
Step history records partition operations for animation
Random pivot produces correct output across 100 seeded runs

title: Unit tests for heap-sort
description: Test the heap sort algorithm with canonical edge cases, verify max-heap property during heapification steps, and validate step recording for the visualization layer.
acceptanceCriteria: All canonical edge cases (empty, single, sorted, reverse, duplicates) pass
Intermediate heap state after build-max-heap satisfies heap property
Step history records each heapify-down operation
Output matches reference sort for 1,000-element random array

title: Unit tests for insertion-sort
description: Test the insertion sort algorithm with canonical edge cases, verify adaptive behavior (near-sorted input runs in near-linear time), and validate step recording.
acceptanceCriteria: All canonical edge cases pass
Near-sorted input (2 swaps away) completes with fewer comparisons than random
Step history records each insertion shift for animation
Stable sort: equal elements preserve original relative order

title: Unit tests for selection-sort
description: Test the selection sort algorithm with canonical edge cases and verify the minimum-finding sweep in each pass is recorded for visualization.
acceptanceCriteria: All canonical edge cases (empty, single, sorted, reverse, duplicates) pass
Comparison count is exactly n*(n-1)/2 for n elements regardless of input order
Step history records each min-scan and swap for animation
Output matches reference sort for random 500-element array

title: Parametric cross-check: all sorting algorithms produce identical output
description: Create a parametric test suite that runs every sorting algorithm against 20 randomly seeded inputs and asserts they all produce identical sorted output. Validates all sort implementations against each other as a consistency oracle.
acceptanceCriteria: Test matrix covers all 6 sorting algorithms x 20 random inputs
Every algorithm produces byte-identical output for each input
Seeded random generator ensures reproducibility across runs
Test fails descriptively if any algorithm diverges, naming the algorithm and input

title: Unit tests for Raft consensus (election, replication, crash, partition)
description: Test the Raft consensus simulation covering leader election (timeout triggers candidacy, majority grants leadership), log replication (entries committed after majority ack), crash recovery (follower rejoins and catches up), and network partition (split-brain prevention).
acceptanceCriteria: Leader election completes within 2 election timeout periods for a 5-node cluster
Log entry is committed only after majority of nodes acknowledge
Crashed follower that rejoins receives missing entries and converges
Network partition with minority leader causes that leader to step down

title: Unit tests for consistent-hash (add/remove, virtual nodes, distribution)
description: Test the consistent hash ring implementation covering node add/remove, virtual node multiplication, key distribution uniformity, and minimal key remapping on topology change.
acceptanceCriteria: Adding a node remaps only keys in the affected range, not all keys
Removing a node reassigns its keys to the next clockwise node
With 150 virtual nodes per physical node, key distribution std-dev < 15% of mean
10,000 keys with 5 nodes: no node holds more than 30% of keys

title: Unit tests for vector-clock (causality, concurrency detection)
description: Test the vector clock implementation covering increment, merge, happens-before comparison, and concurrent event detection across multiple processes.
acceptanceCriteria: Increment on process P increases P's counter by 1, others unchanged
Merge takes element-wise max of two clocks
Happens-before correctly identifies causal ordering (A < B when all A[i] <= B[i])
Concurrent detection returns true when neither clock dominates the other

title: Unit tests for TCP state machine (states, handshake, teardown)
description: Test the TCP state machine simulation covering all 11 states, three-way handshake (SYN, SYN-ACK, ACK), data transfer, and four-way teardown (FIN, ACK, FIN, ACK). Validate invalid transitions are rejected.
acceptanceCriteria: Three-way handshake transitions: CLOSED -> SYN_SENT -> ESTABLISHED
Four-way teardown transitions: ESTABLISHED -> FIN_WAIT_1 -> TIME_WAIT -> CLOSED
Invalid transition (e.g., CLOSED -> ESTABLISHED) throws or returns error
All 11 TCP states are reachable through valid transition sequences

title: Unit tests for TLS handshake simulation (message counts)
description: Test the TLS handshake simulation for TLS 1.2 (2-RTT) and TLS 1.3 (1-RTT) flows. Validate correct message sequence, message count, and cipher suite negotiation.
acceptanceCriteria: TLS 1.2 handshake produces exactly 4 flight messages (2 round trips)
TLS 1.3 handshake produces exactly 2 flight messages (1 round trip)
Cipher suite mismatch triggers handshake failure with descriptive error
Session resumption (0-RTT) skips full handshake when PSK is valid

title: Unit tests for DNS resolution (chain, cache, CNAME)
description: Test the DNS resolution simulation covering iterative resolution chain (root -> TLD -> authoritative), caching with TTL expiry, and CNAME follow through.
acceptanceCriteria: Iterative resolution visits root, TLD, and authoritative servers in order
Cached entries return immediately without network hops until TTL expires
CNAME record triggers a follow-up resolution for the canonical name
Non-existent domain returns NXDOMAIN after full chain traversal

title: Unit tests for scheduling algorithms (FCFS, SJF, RR metrics)
description: Test CPU scheduling algorithm implementations: First Come First Served, Shortest Job First, and Round Robin. Validate average waiting time, turnaround time, and response time against known textbook examples.
acceptanceCriteria: FCFS with processes [24, 3, 3] produces average waiting time of 17.0
SJF (non-preemptive) with known input matches textbook expected output
Round Robin with quantum=4 produces correct Gantt chart and metrics
All algorithms handle single-process and equal-burst-time edge cases

title: Unit tests for page-replacement algorithms (fault counts)
description: Test page replacement algorithm implementations (FIFO, LRU, Optimal) with known reference strings. Validate page fault counts match textbook answers for given frame counts.
acceptanceCriteria: FIFO with reference string [7,0,1,2,0,3,0,4,2,3,0,3,2] and 3 frames yields 15 faults
LRU with same reference string yields correct fault count per textbook
Optimal algorithm produces minimum possible faults for the reference string
Belady's anomaly demonstrated: FIFO with 4 frames can have more faults than 3 frames

title: Unit tests for export/to-json roundtrip
description: Test the JSON export function by serializing a diagram state to JSON and deserializing it back, asserting deep equality. Cover nodes, edges, metadata, and custom properties.
acceptanceCriteria: Roundtrip: toJSON(diagram) -> fromJSON(json) produces deeply equal diagram
Custom node data properties are preserved through serialization
Edge metadata (labels, animated flag) survives roundtrip
Empty diagram (no nodes, no edges) roundtrips without error

title: Unit tests for export/to-mermaid syntax validation
description: Test the Mermaid export function produces valid Mermaid graph syntax. Validate node declarations, edge arrows, subgraph groupings, and that output parses without errors via mermaid.parse().
acceptanceCriteria: Single node exports as valid Mermaid node declaration
Edge between two nodes produces correct arrow syntax (-->)
Node labels with special characters are properly escaped
Generated Mermaid string passes mermaid.parse() without syntax errors

title: Unit tests for export/to-plantuml syntax validation
description: Test the PlantUML export function produces valid PlantUML component diagram syntax. Validate @startuml/@enduml wrapper, component declarations, and relationship arrows.
acceptanceCriteria: Output starts with @startuml and ends with @enduml
Each node maps to a PlantUML component with correct stereotype
Edges produce correct PlantUML arrow syntax (-->)
Empty diagram produces minimal valid PlantUML document

title: Unit tests for export/to-url encode/decode roundtrip
description: Test the URL-based sharing export that encodes diagram state into a compressed URL parameter and decodes it back. Validate roundtrip fidelity, URL length limits, and handling of special characters.
acceptanceCriteria: Roundtrip: toURL(diagram) -> fromURL(url) produces identical diagram state
URL encoding uses base64url-safe characters (no +, /, =)
Large diagram (50 nodes) compresses to under 2,000 characters
Corrupted URL parameter returns descriptive decode error, not crash

title: Unit tests for utils cn() class merging
description: Test the cn() utility function (clsx + tailwind-merge) for correct class concatenation, conflict resolution, and conditional class application.
acceptanceCriteria: cn('px-2', 'px-4') resolves to 'px-4' (last wins for conflicting Tailwind classes)
cn('text-red-500', undefined, false, 'font-bold') skips falsy values
cn('bg-blue-500', { 'bg-red-500': true }) resolves conditional to 'bg-red-500'
Empty call cn() returns empty string

title: Unit tests for playback-controller
description: Test the playback controller that drives algorithm and simulation animations: play, pause, step-forward, step-backward, speed adjustment, and reset. Validate frame index management and callback invocation.
acceptanceCriteria: play() starts interval that increments frame index and calls onFrame callback
pause() stops interval without resetting frame index
step-forward and step-backward change frame index by 1 within bounds
Speed change (0.5x, 1x, 2x, 4x) adjusts interval duration proportionally

title: Component test: BaseNode renders with correct category color and state
description: Test the BaseNode component renders with the correct background/border color for each category (compute, storage, network, etc.), and visual states (default, selected, errored, simulating).
acceptanceCriteria: Each category applies the correct CSS class or inline style for its color
Selected state shows ring/border highlight
Errored state shows red indicator and error icon
Simulating state shows animated pulse or glow effect class

title: Component test: All 9 system design nodes render without crash
description: Smoke test that each of the 9 system design node types (LoadBalancer, Server, Database, Cache, Queue, CDN, Gateway, MicroService, Client) renders without throwing, displays its icon, and accepts required props.
acceptanceCriteria: All 9 node components render without React error boundary triggering
Each node displays its expected icon from the icon library
Label prop is rendered as visible text
Snapshot regression test captures current render output

title: Component test: ComponentPalette search filtering and drag initiation
description: Test the ComponentPalette sidebar component for search/filter functionality and drag event initiation. Validate that typing filters the component list, categories collapse/expand, and dragging sets the correct transfer data.
acceptanceCriteria: Typing 'load' in search field filters to show only LoadBalancer component
Clearing search restores the full component list
Dragging a palette item fires dragStart with correct node type in dataTransfer
Category headers collapse/expand their section on click

title: Component test: PropertiesPanel shows config for selected node
description: Test the PropertiesPanel component displays the correct configuration fields when a node is selected on the canvas. Validate field rendering, value display, and edit interaction.
acceptanceCriteria: No node selected: panel shows placeholder message
Database node selected: shows fields for replicas, sharding, engine type
Editing a numeric field updates the store value on blur/enter
Multiple node selection shows shared properties or 'multiple selected' message

title: Component test: CommandPalette opens, searches, and executes commands
description: Test the CommandPalette modal opens on Cmd+K, filters commands as user types, highlights matched text, and executes the selected command on Enter.
acceptanceCriteria: Palette opens when Cmd+K keyboard shortcut is triggered
Typing filters the command list to matching entries
Arrow keys navigate the filtered list; Enter executes the highlighted command
Escape closes the palette and returns focus to the previous element

title: Component test: ArrayVisualizer renders bars with correct colors
description: Test the ArrayVisualizer component renders bars proportional to array values, applies correct colors for comparison/swap/sorted states, and updates on data change.
acceptanceCriteria: Each bar height is proportional to its value relative to the max
Comparing state applies yellow/amber highlight to active bars
Swapping state applies red highlight to the two bars being swapped
Sorted state applies green to finalized bars

title: Component test: ActivityBar renders all modules and highlights active
description: Test the ActivityBar renders an icon button for every module, highlights the currently active module, and fires the module switch callback on click.
acceptanceCriteria: ActivityBar renders exactly one button per module in the module list
Active module button has a distinct visual indicator (highlight/border)
Clicking an inactive module button fires onModuleChange with correct module ID
Tooltip with module name appears on button hover

title: Component test: WorkspaceLayout respects panel toggles
description: Test the WorkspaceLayout component shows/hides sidebar, properties panel, and bottom panel based on UI store toggle state. Validate layout reflow on toggle.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar visible by default; toggling hides it and canvas expands to fill space
Properties panel toggles independently of sidebar
Bottom panel toggle shows/hides the panel with correct animation class
All three panels hidden: canvas occupies full viewport area

title: Component test: BottomPanel tab switching
description: Test the BottomPanel component switches between tabs (Console, Metrics, Problems, Output), displays correct content for each, and preserves tab state across re-renders.
acceptanceCriteria: Default tab is Console; content area shows console output
Clicking Metrics tab switches content to metrics display
Active tab has visual indicator (underline/highlight)
Tab state is preserved when parent component re-renders

title: Component test: AlgorithmPanel selects algo, generates random input, and runs
description: Test the AlgorithmPanel component allows algorithm selection from dropdown, generates random input array via button, and starts the sorting run. Validate state transitions: idle -> running -> complete.
acceptanceCriteria: Algorithm dropdown lists all 6 sorting algorithms
Generate Random button creates an array of configurable size (default 20)
Run button transitions state from idle to running with progress indicator
Completion resets to idle state with sorted result displayed

title: E2E test: Drag component from palette to canvas
description: Playwright end-to-end test that drags a LoadBalancer component from the palette sidebar and drops it onto the canvas, verifying a new node appears at the drop position.
acceptanceCriteria: Palette sidebar is visible and contains LoadBalancer item
Drag-and-drop action from palette to canvas completes without error
A new LoadBalancer node is visible on the canvas after drop
Node position is within 50px of the drop coordinates

title: E2E test: Connect two nodes with edge
description: Playwright end-to-end test that creates two nodes on the canvas, then drags from the source handle of one to the target handle of the other, verifying an edge is created.
acceptanceCriteria: Two nodes are placed on the canvas programmatically or via drag
Dragging from source handle to target handle creates a visible edge
Edge connects the correct source and target nodes
Edge is selectable and deletable via keyboard

title: E2E test: Run sorting algorithm and verify animation
description: Playwright end-to-end test that navigates to the Algorithm module, selects bubble sort, generates a random array, runs the sort, and verifies the animation completes with a sorted result.
acceptanceCriteria: Algorithm module is accessible and displays the sorting interface
Selecting bubble sort and clicking Run starts the animation
Animation bars visually change position during execution
Final state shows bars in ascending order from left to right

title: E2E test: Switch between all modules
description: Playwright end-to-end test that clicks through every module in the ActivityBar and verifies each module's workspace loads without errors.
acceptanceCriteria: Each ActivityBar module button is clickable
Clicking a module renders its corresponding workspace content
No console errors are thrown during any module switch
Round-trip back to the first module restores its previous state

title: E2E test: Command palette open and execute
description: Playwright end-to-end test that opens the command palette via Cmd+K, types a command query, selects a result, and verifies the command action executes.
acceptanceCriteria: Cmd+K opens the command palette overlay
Typing filters the list to matching commands
Pressing Enter on a highlighted command executes it
Palette closes after execution and focus returns to the workspace

title: E2E test: Load template from gallery
description: Playwright end-to-end test that opens the template gallery, selects a predefined system design template, and verifies the canvas populates with the template's nodes and edges.
acceptanceCriteria: Template gallery modal opens and displays available templates
Clicking a template card loads its diagram onto the canvas
Loaded diagram contains the expected number of nodes and edges
Canvas viewport auto-fits to show all loaded nodes

title: E2E test: Export diagram to JSON
description: Playwright end-to-end test that creates a diagram with nodes and edges, triggers the Export to JSON action, and verifies the downloaded file contains valid JSON matching the diagram state.
acceptanceCriteria: Export action triggers a file download with .json extension
Downloaded JSON parses without errors
JSON contains the correct node count and edge count matching the canvas
Re-importing the exported JSON reproduces the original diagram

title: GitHub Actions CI workflow (lint, typecheck, test, build, E2E)
description: Create a comprehensive GitHub Actions CI pipeline that runs on every push and PR. Steps: install dependencies, lint (ESLint + Prettier check), typecheck (tsc --noEmit), unit tests (Vitest), build (next build), and E2E tests (Playwright). Cache node_modules and .next/cache between runs.
acceptanceCriteria: CI workflow triggers on push to main and on all PRs
Pipeline stages run sequentially: lint -> typecheck -> test -> build -> E2E
Failure in any stage stops the pipeline and reports the failing step
Node modules and Next.js cache are cached between runs for speed

title: Bundle size monitoring with size-limit and PR comments
description: Configure size-limit to track main bundle and per-page chunk sizes. Add a GitHub Action that compares bundle size against the base branch and posts a comment on the PR with size deltas.
acceptanceCriteria: .size-limit.json defines limits for main bundle (<250KB) and per-page chunks (<100KB)
CI step runs size-limit check and fails if limits are exceeded
PR comment shows size delta table comparing current vs base branch
Historical size data is tracked for trend analysis

title: Vercel preview deploys per PR
description: Configure Vercel project integration so that every pull request automatically receives a unique preview deployment URL. Add the preview URL as a PR comment via the Vercel GitHub integration.
acceptanceCriteria: Every PR triggers a Vercel preview deployment
Preview URL is posted as a comment on the PR within 3 minutes of push
Preview deploys use the same environment variables as staging
Closing the PR cleans up the preview deployment

title: Visual regression testing workflow with Playwright screenshots
description: Set up a visual regression testing workflow that captures Playwright screenshots of key pages/components, compares against baseline snapshots, and flags visual diffs in PRs.
acceptanceCriteria: Playwright test captures screenshots of at least 5 key views
Baseline screenshots are committed and versioned in the repo
Visual diff above threshold (0.1%) fails the CI check
PR comment includes visual diff images for easy review

title: Lighthouse CI (performance > 90, accessibility > 95)
description: Integrate Lighthouse CI into the GitHub Actions pipeline to audit performance, accessibility, best practices, and SEO scores on every PR. Fail the build if performance drops below 90 or accessibility below 95.
acceptanceCriteria: Lighthouse CI runs against the Vercel preview deploy URL
Performance score must be >= 90 to pass
Accessibility score must be >= 95 to pass
Lighthouse report is uploaded as a CI artifact for review

title: Dependency audit weekly cron job
description: Set up a weekly GitHub Actions cron workflow that runs npm audit and checks for known vulnerabilities. Automatically opens an issue if critical or high severity vulnerabilities are found.
acceptanceCriteria: Cron job runs every Monday at 09:00 UTC
npm audit runs and captures critical/high vulnerability count
If vulnerabilities found, a GitHub issue is auto-created with details
No duplicate issues are created for already-reported vulnerabilities

title: WASM build step in CI (conditional on Rust engine)
description: Add a conditional CI step that builds the Rust-based WASM engine using wasm-pack when Rust source files are modified. Skip the step entirely when no Rust files are changed.
acceptanceCriteria: WASM build step only runs when files in the wasm/ directory are modified
wasm-pack build produces a valid .wasm artifact
Built WASM artifact is cached for use by downstream E2E tests
Step is skipped with clear log message when no Rust changes detected

title: Branch protection rules (require status checks, reviews)
description: Configure GitHub branch protection rules for the main branch requiring passing CI status checks, at least one approving review, and no force pushes.
acceptanceCriteria: Main branch requires all CI status checks to pass before merge
At least 1 approving review is required for PRs to main
Force pushes to main are disabled
Branch protection rules are documented in CONTRIBUTING.md

title: Dockerfile for production (multi-stage build)
description: Create a multi-stage Dockerfile for production deployment: stage 1 installs dependencies and builds the Next.js app, stage 2 copies only the production output for a minimal runtime image.
acceptanceCriteria: Multi-stage build produces a runtime image under 200MB
Production image runs the Next.js standalone server on port 3000
Non-root user is used in the runtime stage
Health check endpoint is configured in the Dockerfile

title: docker-compose.yml for local development (app + Postgres)
description: Create a docker-compose.yml that spins up the Next.js application and a PostgreSQL database for local development. Include volume mounts for hot reload and persistent database storage.
acceptanceCriteria: docker-compose up starts both the app and Postgres containers
App container hot-reloads on source file changes via volume mount
Postgres data persists across container restarts via named volume
Environment variables are sourced from .env.local with docker-compose override

title: React.memo ALL custom node and edge components
description: Wrap every custom React Flow node component and edge component with React.memo to prevent unnecessary re-renders when unrelated canvas state changes. Add custom comparison functions where props include objects.
acceptanceCriteria: All custom node components (9 types) are wrapped with React.memo
All custom edge components are wrapped with React.memo
Custom comparator used for components with object/array props
React DevTools Profiler shows reduced re-renders during canvas pan/zoom

title: Define nodeTypes/edgeTypes OUTSIDE components as static references
description: Move the nodeTypes and edgeTypes objects outside of the React Flow wrapper component to module-level constants. This prevents React Flow from re-registering types on every parent render.
acceptanceCriteria: nodeTypes object is defined at module scope, not inside a component
edgeTypes object is defined at module scope, not inside a component
React Flow does not log 'node type changed' warnings in console
No functional regression in node/edge rendering

title: Batch Zustand updates for diagram loading
description: When loading a diagram (from template, import, or undo), batch all Zustand state updates (nodes, edges, viewport, metadata) into a single React render cycle using ReactDOM.unstable_batchedUpdates or Zustand's built-in batching.
acceptanceCriteria: Loading a 50-node diagram triggers at most 2 React render cycles
No intermediate visual flash of partial diagram during load
Performance profiler shows single paint after diagram load
Undo/redo operations also batch their state restoration

title: Level-of-Detail rendering at zoom thresholds
description: Implement level-of-detail (LOD) rendering for canvas nodes: at zoom < 0.5 show simplified rectangles with label only, at zoom 0.5-1.0 show standard view, at zoom > 1.0 show detailed view with metrics and config.
acceptanceCriteria: Zoom < 0.5: nodes render as colored rectangles with label text only
Zoom 0.5-1.0: nodes render standard view with icon and label
Zoom > 1.0: nodes render detailed view with inline metrics
Transitions between LOD levels are smooth with no layout flicker

title: Disable animated edges when node count exceeds 200
description: Add a performance guard that automatically disables edge animations (animated dashes, particle flow) when the canvas contains more than 200 nodes. Show a toast informing the user.
acceptanceCriteria: Edge animations are disabled when node count > 200
Toast notification informs user that animations were disabled for performance
User can manually re-enable animations via settings override
Animations are automatically re-enabled when node count drops below 150

title: Lazy-load Monaco editor via dynamic import
description: Convert the Monaco editor import to a Next.js dynamic() import with ssr: false so it is only loaded when the user navigates to a code editing view. Show a skeleton loader during the async load.
acceptanceCriteria: Monaco editor chunk is not in the main bundle (verified via bundle analyzer)
Dynamic import loads Monaco only when the editor panel is first opened
Skeleton loader displays during the async load with correct dimensions
Editor is fully functional after lazy load (syntax highlighting, intellisense)

title: Lazy-load WASM chunks with cache-first service worker
description: Implement lazy loading for WASM simulation engine chunks using dynamic import. Register a service worker strategy that caches WASM files with a cache-first approach keyed by content hash.
acceptanceCriteria: WASM chunks are loaded on-demand, not in initial bundle
Service worker intercepts WASM fetch and serves from cache if available
Cache key includes content hash for correct invalidation on update
First load fetches from network; subsequent loads serve from cache

title: Tree-shake D3 (import only d3-force submodules)
description: Replace the full D3 import with targeted submodule imports (d3-force, d3-scale, d3-shape) to enable tree-shaking. Remove the top-level 'd3' dependency from package.json.
acceptanceCriteria: No import from 'd3' top-level package exists in the codebase
Only d3-force, d3-scale, and d3-shape are in package.json dependencies
Bundle size for D3-related code decreases by at least 60%
All force-directed layout functionality still works correctly

title: Zustand atomic selectors with useShallow where needed
description: Audit all Zustand useStore() calls and replace broad selectors with atomic selectors that pick individual fields. Use useShallow() for selectors that return objects to prevent unnecessary re-renders.
acceptanceCriteria: No component selects the entire store state (e.g., useCanvasStore())
Object-returning selectors use useShallow for shallow comparison
React DevTools Profiler shows reduced re-render count on node selection
All existing functionality works identically after selector refactoring

title: Configure .size-limit.json (main <250KB, page <100KB)
description: Create a .size-limit.json configuration file that enforces bundle size budgets: main JS bundle under 250KB gzipped, individual page chunks under 100KB gzipped. Integrate with the CI pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: Main bundle limit set to 250KB gzipped in .size-limit.json
Per-page chunk limit set to 100KB gzipped
pnpm run size-limit passes for current build
CI step fails build if any limit is exceeded

title: React Flow stress test (50/100/200/500 nodes FPS)
description: Create a benchmark test that renders React Flow canvases with 50, 100, 200, and 500 nodes while measuring frames per second during pan/zoom interactions. Establish FPS baselines and regression thresholds.
acceptanceCriteria: Benchmark renders 50, 100, 200, and 500 node canvases
FPS is measured during continuous pan operation for 5 seconds each
50 nodes: >= 60 FPS; 100 nodes: >= 50 FPS; 200 nodes: >= 30 FPS
Results are logged to a JSON file for trend tracking

title: Memory budget test (<80MB per module)
description: Create a Playwright-based memory test that navigates to each module, performs typical interactions, and measures JS heap size. Assert that no module exceeds 80MB heap usage.
acceptanceCriteria: Memory test visits each of the primary modules
JS heap size is captured after 30 seconds of interaction per module
No module exceeds 80MB JS heap usage
Memory leak detection: heap after cleanup is within 10% of initial heap

title: Web Vitals monitoring (LCP <2s, INP <150ms, CLS <0.05)
description: Integrate web-vitals library to capture and report Core Web Vitals (LCP, INP, CLS) in production. Add a performance monitoring panel in the bottom bar for development mode.
acceptanceCriteria: web-vitals library captures LCP, INP, and CLS on every page load
Development mode shows a Web Vitals overlay with live metrics
LCP target: < 2 seconds on initial load
CLS target: < 0.05; INP target: < 150ms

title: OffscreenCanvas for minimap worker
description: Move the minimap rendering to an OffscreenCanvas in a Web Worker so minimap updates do not block the main thread during complex canvas interactions.
acceptanceCriteria: Minimap rendering runs in a dedicated Web Worker via OffscreenCanvas
Main thread FPS is unaffected by minimap updates
Minimap reflects canvas state changes within 100ms
Graceful fallback to main-thread rendering if OffscreenCanvas is unsupported

title: Particle path pre-computation cache
description: Pre-compute and cache the SVG path data for edge particle animations so that particle positions are looked up from a cache rather than re-calculated every animation frame.
acceptanceCriteria: Edge path data is computed once on edge creation and cached by edge ID
Particle position lookup uses cached path length and getPointAtLength
Cache is invalidated when an edge's source or target node moves
Profiler shows at least 30% reduction in animation frame cost for 100-edge diagrams

title: Add <nav> landmark to ActivityBar
description: Wrap the ActivityBar component content in a <nav> landmark element with an aria-label of 'Module navigation' so screen readers can identify it as the primary navigation region.
acceptanceCriteria: ActivityBar renders a <nav> element as its root or wrapping element
aria-label='Module navigation' is present on the <nav> element
Screen reader announces the navigation landmark when entering the region
No duplicate <nav> landmarks exist on the same page

title: Add <main> landmark to canvas area
description: Wrap the primary canvas/workspace area in a <main> landmark element with an aria-label indicating the current module name.
acceptanceCriteria: Canvas area renders inside a <main> element
aria-label dynamically reflects the active module (e.g., 'System Design workspace')
Only one <main> landmark exists in the page DOM
Skip-to-content link targets this <main> element

title: Add <aside> landmarks to sidebar and properties panels
description: Wrap the component palette sidebar and properties panel in <aside> landmark elements with descriptive aria-labels.
acceptanceCriteria: Component palette sidebar renders inside <aside aria-label='Component palette'>
Properties panel renders inside <aside aria-label='Node properties'>
Both landmarks are announced by screen readers when focused
Landmarks are removed from the DOM when their panels are hidden

title: Add heading hierarchy (h1 for module, h2 for panels)
description: Establish a proper heading hierarchy throughout the application: h1 for the current module name, h2 for panel titles (sidebar, properties, bottom panel sections), h3 for subsections within panels.
acceptanceCriteria: Each page has exactly one <h1> displaying the active module name
Panel titles use <h2> elements
Subsections within panels use <h3> elements
No heading levels are skipped (no h1 -> h3 without h2)

title: Add role=tablist/role=tab to BottomPanel tabs
description: Apply WAI-ARIA tab pattern to the BottomPanel: role='tablist' on the tab container, role='tab' on each tab button, role='tabpanel' on each content area, with proper aria-selected and aria-controls attributes.
acceptanceCriteria: Tab container has role='tablist'
Each tab button has role='tab' with aria-selected reflecting active state
Each tab panel has role='tabpanel' with aria-labelledby pointing to its tab
Arrow keys navigate between tabs per WAI-ARIA tabs pattern

title: Add aria-selected to active module button in ActivityBar
description: Add aria-selected='true' to the currently active module button in the ActivityBar and aria-selected='false' to all inactive buttons. Apply role='tab' to each button if using tablist pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Active module button has aria-selected='true'
All other module buttons have aria-selected='false'
Screen reader announces the selected state on focus
aria-selected updates immediately on module switch

title: Add aria-label to all ActivityBar buttons
description: Ensure every icon button in the ActivityBar has a descriptive aria-label (e.g., 'System Design', 'Algorithms', 'Settings') since they display only icons without visible text.
acceptanceCriteria: Every ActivityBar button has an aria-label matching its module name
Screen reader announces the label when the button receives focus
No unlabelled interactive elements in the ActivityBar per axe audit
Labels are consistent with tooltip text

title: Add role=dialog and aria-modal to CommandPalette
description: Apply role='dialog' and aria-modal='true' to the CommandPalette overlay. Add aria-labelledby pointing to the search input label and aria-describedby for usage instructions.
acceptanceCriteria: CommandPalette has role='dialog' and aria-modal='true'
aria-labelledby references a label element (visible or screen-reader-only)
Screen reader announces 'Command palette' when opened
Escape key closes the dialog and restores previous focus

title: Add aria-live=polite for simulation state changes
description: Create an aria-live='polite' region that announces simulation state changes (started, paused, stopped, error) to screen reader users without interrupting their current focus.
acceptanceCriteria: A visually hidden aria-live='polite' region exists in the DOM
Simulation start announces 'Simulation started'
Simulation pause announces 'Simulation paused'
Simulation error announces the error message

title: Focus trapping in CommandPalette
description: Implement focus trapping within the CommandPalette dialog so that Tab and Shift+Tab cycle only through the palette's interactive elements (search input, result list) while the dialog is open.
acceptanceCriteria: Tab from the last focusable element wraps to the first
Shift+Tab from the first focusable element wraps to the last
Focus does not escape to elements behind the modal overlay
Focus moves to the search input when the palette opens

title: Focus trapping in ExportDialog
description: Implement focus trapping within the Export dialog so that keyboard navigation is confined to the dialog's interactive elements while it is open.
acceptanceCriteria: Tab cycles through export format options and action buttons only
Shift+Tab wraps from the first element to the last
Focus moves to the first interactive element when dialog opens
Escape closes the dialog and restores focus to the trigger button

title: Skip-to-main-content link
description: Add a visually hidden skip-to-main-content link as the first focusable element in the page that becomes visible on focus and jumps to the <main> landmark.
acceptanceCriteria: Skip link is the first focusable element when tabbing from page top
Skip link becomes visible when focused (positioned on screen)
Activating the link moves focus to the <main> element
Skip link is hidden again after activation or blur

title: Keyboard alternative for palette drag-drop (Enter to add at center)
description: Add a keyboard-accessible alternative to drag-and-drop for the component palette: pressing Enter or Space on a palette item adds the component to the canvas center. Announce the action via aria-live.
acceptanceCriteria: Pressing Enter on a palette component adds it to canvas center
aria-live region announces 'LoadBalancer added to canvas' (or similar)
Added node receives focus on the canvas for immediate keyboard interaction
Palette item shows keyboard hint text (e.g., 'Press Enter to add')

title: Arrow key navigation in ActivityBar
description: Implement roving tabindex pattern for ActivityBar buttons: Arrow Up/Down moves focus between module buttons, Home/End jump to first/last, and only the focused button is in the tab order.
acceptanceCriteria: Arrow Down moves focus to the next module button
Arrow Up moves focus to the previous module button
Home moves focus to the first button; End to the last
Only one button has tabIndex=0 at any time (roving tabindex pattern)

title: Fix --foreground-subtle contrast ratio to meet AA 4.5:1
description: Audit and fix the --foreground-subtle CSS custom property color value to ensure it meets WCAG AA minimum contrast ratio of 4.5:1 against the background color in both dark and light themes.
acceptanceCriteria: Dark theme: --foreground-subtle achieves >= 4.5:1 ratio against --background
Light theme: --foreground-subtle achieves >= 4.5:1 ratio against --background
Verified with axe DevTools or contrast checker tool
No visual regression: text remains readable and aesthetically consistent

title: Fix ArrayVisualizer 8px text to meet minimum size requirements
description: Increase the ArrayVisualizer index/value labels from 8px to at least 12px to meet WCAG minimum text size guidelines. Adjust layout to accommodate larger text without overlapping bars.
acceptanceCriteria: All text in ArrayVisualizer is at least 12px font size
Text remains readable at all supported container widths
No text overlaps with adjacent bars or labels
Visual appearance remains clean and proportional

title: CanvasDescription component (text summary of diagram)
description: Create a screen-reader-only CanvasDescription component that provides a textual summary of the current diagram (node count, node types, connection summary) for users who cannot see the visual canvas.
acceptanceCriteria: Component renders a visually hidden text description of the diagram
Description includes: total node count, list of node types, total edge count
Description updates when nodes/edges are added or removed
Accessible via screen reader when navigating the canvas region

title: NodeListPanel (flat navigable alternative to spatial canvas)
description: Create a NodeListPanel that presents all canvas nodes in a flat, keyboard-navigable list as an alternative to the spatial canvas for screen reader users. Each list item shows node type, label, and connection count.
acceptanceCriteria: Panel renders a list of all nodes with type, label, and connection info
List items are keyboard navigable with arrow keys
Selecting a list item selects the corresponding node on the canvas
Panel is accessible via a toggle button or keyboard shortcut

title: Keyboard node selection (Tab to cycle, Enter to select)
description: Implement keyboard node selection on the canvas: Tab cycles focus through nodes in DOM order, Enter selects the focused node, and the focused node shows a visible focus ring.
acceptanceCriteria: Tab moves focus to the next node on the canvas
Shift+Tab moves focus to the previous node
Enter selects the focused node (opens properties panel)
Focused node displays a visible 2px focus ring meeting 3:1 contrast

title: Keyboard node movement (Arrow keys + snap to grid)
description: Allow users to move the currently selected node using Arrow keys. Each press moves the node by one grid unit. Hold Shift for 10x movement speed. Announce new position via aria-live.
acceptanceCriteria: Arrow keys move the selected node by one grid unit (10px default)
Shift+Arrow moves by 10 grid units for faster repositioning
Node snaps to grid after each movement
aria-live announces 'Node moved to row X, column Y' after movement

title: Canvas screen reader announcements (node added/deleted, connection created)
description: Create a centralized announcement system for canvas operations that updates an aria-live region whenever nodes are added, deleted, or connections are created or removed.
acceptanceCriteria: Adding a node announces 'LoadBalancer node added' (with type name)
Deleting a node announces 'Database node deleted'
Creating a connection announces 'Connection from Server to Database created'
Announcements do not interrupt ongoing screen reader speech (polite level)

title: Colorblind-safe palette option (IBM Design or Wong)
description: Add a colorblind-safe color palette option using the IBM Design colorblind-safe palette or Wong's colorblind-safe palette. Apply it to all category colors, chart colors, and status indicators.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings panel includes a 'Colorblind-safe colors' toggle
Toggle applies a verified colorblind-safe palette to all category colors
Chart/visualization colors also switch to the safe palette
All 8 color-vision-deficiency types (protanopia, deuteranopia, etc.) are distinguishable

title: Respect prefers-reduced-motion: disable particles and animations
description: Add a CSS media query and JavaScript check for prefers-reduced-motion: reduce. When active, disable particle animations on edges, sorting bar transitions, simulation animations, and any decorative motion.
acceptanceCriteria: CSS transitions/animations are disabled when prefers-reduced-motion: reduce is active
Edge particle animations are stopped in JavaScript
Sorting visualizer uses instant bar repositioning instead of animation
A manual toggle in settings also controls this behavior

title: Respect prefers-contrast: more (high contrast theme)
description: Add support for the prefers-contrast: more media query by increasing border widths, strengthening text contrast, and adding explicit outlines to interactive elements for users who need higher contrast.
acceptanceCriteria: All text achieves >= 7:1 contrast ratio when prefers-contrast: more is active
Borders increase to 2px and use solid high-contrast colors
Interactive elements have explicit outlines (not just background changes)
Focus indicators are at least 3px with high contrast

title: Forced-colors support (Windows High Contrast)
description: Add forced-colors media query support to ensure the application is usable in Windows High Contrast mode. Use system colors for text, backgrounds, and borders; avoid CSS that overrides forced colors.
acceptanceCriteria: All text uses CanvasText system color in forced-colors mode
Backgrounds use Canvas system color
Interactive element borders use ButtonText system color
No forced-color-adjust: none on essential content

title: 44px minimum touch targets on playback controls
description: Ensure all playback control buttons (play, pause, stop, step-forward, step-backward, speed) meet the 44x44px minimum touch target size per WCAG 2.5.8.
acceptanceCriteria: All playback buttons are at least 44x44px in clickable area
Visual size may be smaller if padding extends the touch target
Touch targets do not overlap with adjacent controls
Verified on mobile viewport (375px width)

title: 44px touch targets on connection handles
description: Increase the interactive area of React Flow connection handles to at least 44x44px for touch accessibility, while keeping the visual dot small. Use invisible padding to extend the hit area.
acceptanceCriteria: Connection handle hit area is at least 44x44px
Visual handle dot remains 8-12px for aesthetics
Invisible padding extends the interactive area without layout shift
Touch connection initiation works reliably on mobile devices

title: Fix focus-visible rings on all interactive elements
description: Audit and fix focus-visible outlines on all buttons, links, inputs, and custom interactive elements. Ensure a consistent 2px outline with at least 3:1 contrast ratio against adjacent colors.
acceptanceCriteria: All interactive elements show a visible focus ring on :focus-visible
Focus ring is 2px solid with at least 3:1 contrast ratio
Focus ring offset prevents overlap with element borders
axe audit reports zero 'focus-visible' related violations

title: Restore focus on dialog close
description: Ensure that when any dialog or modal is closed (CommandPalette, ExportDialog, TemplateGallery, etc.), focus returns to the element that triggered the dialog opening.
acceptanceCriteria: Closing CommandPalette restores focus to the element focused before it opened
Closing ExportDialog restores focus to the Export button
Closing TemplateGallery restores focus to the Template button
Focus restoration works for both Escape key and close button dismissal

title: Add aria-label to all form inputs
description: Audit all form inputs (text fields, selects, sliders, checkboxes) in the application and ensure each has either a visible <label> element or an aria-label attribute.
acceptanceCriteria: Every <input>, <select>, and <textarea> has a programmatic label
Visible labels use htmlFor matching the input id
Icon-only inputs (e.g., search) use aria-label
axe audit reports zero 'label' related violations

title: Add viewport meta tag in layout.tsx
description: Ensure the root layout.tsx includes the proper viewport meta tag with width=device-width, initial-scale=1, and viewport-fit=cover for correct mobile rendering and safe area support.
acceptanceCriteria: Viewport meta tag present: width=device-width, initial-scale=1
viewport-fit=cover included for notched device support
No user-scalable=no (allow pinch zoom per WCAG)
Page renders correctly on mobile without horizontal scroll

title: Responsive breakpoints (hide labels <768px, collapse sidebar <1024px)
description: Implement responsive breakpoints: at <768px hide ActivityBar text labels (icons only), at <1024px auto-collapse the sidebar to icon-only mode, and at <640px switch to mobile layout.
acceptanceCriteria: Below 768px: ActivityBar shows icons only, no text labels
Below 1024px: sidebar collapses automatically to icon-only strip
Below 640px: layout switches to full mobile mode with bottom navigation
Breakpoint transitions are smooth with no layout jump

title: BottomSheet component for mobile sidebar content
description: Create a BottomSheet component that slides up from the bottom of the screen on mobile to display sidebar content (component palette, properties panel). Support drag-to-dismiss and snap points (25%, 50%, 90%).
acceptanceCriteria: BottomSheet slides up from the screen bottom with spring animation
Drag handle allows pull-down to dismiss
Snap points at 25%, 50%, and 90% of viewport height
Background content is dimmed and scroll-locked while sheet is open

title: FloatingActionButton for mobile quick actions
description: Create a FloatingActionButton (FAB) component for mobile that provides quick access to common actions (add component, undo, redo). Expands into a speed-dial menu on tap.
acceptanceCriteria: FAB renders as a 56px circular button in the bottom-right corner
Tapping expands a speed-dial menu with 3-5 action icons
Speed-dial items have labels and are touch-accessible
FAB is hidden when BottomSheet is fully expanded

title: Horizontal bottom navigation bar on mobile
description: Create a mobile bottom navigation bar that replaces the vertical ActivityBar on small screens. Display 4-5 primary module icons with labels in a horizontal tab bar fixed to the bottom.
acceptanceCriteria: Bottom nav renders at screen widths < 640px, replacing the ActivityBar
Displays 4-5 primary modules as icon + label tab items
Active module is visually highlighted
Safe area inset padding applied below the nav on notched devices

title: Pinch-to-zoom on canvas
description: Implement pinch-to-zoom gesture support on the canvas for mobile devices. Map two-finger pinch/spread gestures to React Flow's zoom controls with smooth momentum.
acceptanceCriteria: Two-finger pinch gesture zooms out on the canvas
Two-finger spread gesture zooms in on the canvas
Zoom center point is between the two touch points
Zoom respects min/max zoom limits configured in the viewport store

title: Two-finger pan on canvas
description: Implement two-finger pan gesture on the canvas for mobile devices. Single-finger touch should select/drag nodes, while two-finger touch pans the viewport.
acceptanceCriteria: Two-finger drag pans the canvas viewport
Single-finger drag selects or moves individual nodes
Pan momentum continues briefly after finger lift for smooth feel
Pan works correctly in combination with pinch-to-zoom

title: Long-press context menu on nodes
description: Implement a long-press gesture (500ms) on canvas nodes that opens a context menu with options: Edit, Duplicate, Delete, Connect To. Use haptic feedback where supported.
acceptanceCriteria: Long press (500ms) on a node opens a context menu
Menu includes Edit, Duplicate, Delete, and Connect To options
Haptic feedback fires on menu open (where navigator.vibrate is supported)
Tapping outside the menu dismisses it

title: Properties panel as sliding sheet on mobile
description: On mobile viewports, display the properties panel as a sliding sheet from the right edge instead of a fixed sidebar. Include a drag handle and swipe-to-dismiss gesture.
acceptanceCriteria: Properties panel renders as a right-sliding sheet on mobile (<768px)
Sheet slides in when a node is selected, slides out on deselect
Swipe-right gesture dismisses the sheet
Sheet width is 85% of viewport width

title: CommandPalette full-width on mobile
description: Make the CommandPalette render full-width on mobile viewports (<640px) with larger touch targets and a full-screen overlay instead of the centered popover.
acceptanceCriteria: CommandPalette renders full-width on screens < 640px
Search input and result items have 48px minimum height for touch
Overlay covers the full screen with dimmed background
Virtual keyboard does not obscure the search results

title: Safe area insets for notched devices
description: Apply env(safe-area-inset-*) CSS values to all fixed-position elements (bottom nav, FAB, bottom sheet) to prevent content from being obscured by device notches and home indicators.
acceptanceCriteria: Bottom navigation includes padding-bottom: env(safe-area-inset-bottom)
FAB position accounts for safe-area-inset-right and safe-area-inset-bottom
Header accounts for safe-area-inset-top
Layout renders correctly on iPhone (notch) and Android (punch-hole) simulators

title: Set touch-action: none on canvas to prevent swipe-back
description: Apply touch-action: none to the canvas container element to prevent browser swipe-back navigation and other default touch behaviors that interfere with canvas gestures.
acceptanceCriteria: Canvas element has touch-action: none CSS property
Swiping on the canvas does not trigger browser back navigation
Pull-to-refresh is disabled on the canvas area
Touch gestures (pan, zoom) within the canvas work without interference

title: Template gallery responsive grid
description: Make the template gallery use a responsive CSS grid that adjusts columns based on viewport width: 1 column on mobile (<640px), 2 columns on tablet (640-1024px), 3 columns on desktop (>1024px).
acceptanceCriteria: Mobile (<640px): single column layout with full-width template cards
Tablet (640-1024px): 2-column grid layout
Desktop (>1024px): 3-column grid layout
Card aspect ratio remains consistent across breakpoints

title: useMediaQuery hook
description: Create a reusable useMediaQuery React hook that listens to CSS media queries and returns a boolean. Support SSR by returning a default value during server rendering.
acceptanceCriteria: useMediaQuery('(max-width: 768px)') returns true on mobile viewports
Hook updates reactively when viewport is resized across the breakpoint
SSR-safe: returns configurable default during server render (no hydration mismatch)
Listener is properly cleaned up on unmount

title: Mobile advisory message for complex editing
description: Display a dismissable advisory message on mobile viewports informing users that complex diagram editing is best experienced on desktop. Show only once per session and respect a 'Don't show again' preference.
acceptanceCriteria: Message appears on first mobile visit to the diagram editor
Message includes 'Best experienced on desktop' text and a dismiss button
'Don't show again' checkbox persists preference to localStorage
Message does not appear on tablet (>=768px) or desktop viewports

title: Web app manifest (name, icons, theme_color, display: standalone)
description: Create a web app manifest.json with application name, short name, description, icon references, theme color matching the Architex violet brand, background color, and display mode set to standalone.
acceptanceCriteria: manifest.json includes name, short_name, and description
theme_color matches the Architex primary violet (#7C3AED)
display is set to 'standalone'
start_url is set to '/' with scope '/'

title: PWA icon set (192x192, 512x512, maskable, apple-touch-icon)
description: Generate and add the required PWA icon set: 192x192 and 512x512 for Android, maskable variant with safe zone, and apple-touch-icon for iOS. All icons should use the Architex logo.
acceptanceCriteria: 192x192 and 512x512 PNG icons present in public/icons/
Maskable icon variant with safe-zone padding included
apple-touch-icon (180x180) present in public/
manifest.json references all icons with correct sizes and purposes

title: Service Worker with Workbox (app shell precache, API network-first)
description: Set up a service worker using Workbox with two strategies: precache the app shell (HTML, CSS, JS) for offline availability, and use a network-first strategy for API/data requests with cache fallback.
acceptanceCriteria: App shell (HTML, CSS, critical JS) is precached on service worker install
API requests use network-first with 3-second timeout, falling back to cache
Static assets use cache-first strategy
Service worker registers successfully in production builds

title: Offline fallback page
description: Create a dedicated offline fallback page that the service worker serves when the user is offline and the requested page is not cached. Include the Architex logo, a friendly message, and a retry button.
acceptanceCriteria: Offline page displays the Architex logo and 'You are offline' message
Retry button attempts to reload the original requested URL
Page is precached by the service worker for guaranteed availability
Design matches the Architex dark theme and brand colors

title: Font caching (Geist Sans, Geist Mono — cache-first)
description: Configure the service worker to cache Geist Sans and Geist Mono font files using a cache-first strategy with a dedicated font cache. Set appropriate cache expiration.
acceptanceCriteria: Font files are served from cache after first load
Dedicated 'fonts' cache stores Geist Sans and Geist Mono files
Cache expiration set to 1 year (fonts are versioned via URL)
Offline mode displays correct fonts from cache

title: WASM chunk caching (cache-first with versioned keys)
description: Configure the service worker to cache WASM simulation engine chunks using a cache-first strategy. Cache key includes the content hash for correct invalidation when the WASM binary is updated.
acceptanceCriteria: WASM files are intercepted and cached by the service worker
Cache key includes content hash for versioned invalidation
Subsequent loads serve WASM from cache (verified via Network panel)
Old WASM versions are evicted when new versions are cached

title: Install prompt UX (custom banner after engagement criteria)
description: Implement a custom PWA install prompt that appears after the user meets engagement criteria (visited 3+ times or spent 5+ minutes). Intercept the beforeinstallprompt event and show a branded banner.
acceptanceCriteria: beforeinstallprompt event is intercepted and deferred
Custom install banner appears after 3 visits or 5 minutes of use
Banner matches Architex brand with 'Install App' CTA button
Dismissing the banner records preference (no re-prompt for 30 days)

title: Update detection toast (Update available, reload)
description: Detect when a new service worker version is available and show a toast notification prompting the user to reload. Clicking 'Update' activates the waiting service worker and reloads the page.
acceptanceCriteria: Toast appears when navigator.serviceWorker detects a waiting worker
Toast message reads 'Update available' with a 'Reload' action button
Clicking Reload calls skipWaiting on the new worker and reloads
Toast auto-dismisses after 30 seconds if user takes no action

title: Background sync for offline diagram saves
description: Implement Background Sync API integration so that diagram save operations made while offline are queued and automatically synced when connectivity is restored.
acceptanceCriteria: Offline save operations are queued in IndexedDB
Background Sync event fires when connectivity is restored
Queued saves are processed in order and synced to the server
User receives a toast notification when offline saves are synced

title: Layout.tsx manifest link and Apple meta tags
description: Add the manifest link and Apple-specific meta tags to the root layout.tsx: apple-mobile-web-app-capable, apple-mobile-web-app-status-bar-style, and apple-touch-icon link.
acceptanceCriteria: <link rel='manifest' href='/manifest.json'> present in <head>
apple-mobile-web-app-capable meta tag set to 'yes'
apple-mobile-web-app-status-bar-style set to 'black-translucent'
apple-touch-icon link references the 180x180 icon
